rulings.Case Title: JAMES BARAGAR et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Navistar, Inc.¿S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 22, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Navistar, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present evidence creating a triable issue regarding the asbestos content of any product attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to the references to Mr. Shelden¿s deposition, the portions of Mr. Shelden¿s declaration regarding asbestos content and the declaration of Mr. Cohen are sustained. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: EDDIE STEWART VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 22, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Lehigh Southwest Cement Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden that Shellie Stewart is incompetent as a witness. The court reaffirms the prior tentative ruling that the declarations of Randy George and Shellie Stewart create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to an asbestos-containing product (monolith asbestos gun cement) attributable to defendant. The objections to the declaration of Mr. George and Mr. Stewart are overruled. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production as to issues 2 and 3. =(220/HEK)
